LeanCTX

Discord Get Started
Features Getting Started Installation Token Dense Dialect CLI Reference Changelog
Discord GitHub
Get Started

Your AI agent wastes tokens on context it already has. Stop it.

LeanCTX is a hybrid context optimizer — a transparent shell hook with 50+ compression patterns (git, npm, cargo, docker, kubectl, gh, and many more), plus an MCP server with 8 tools for cached reads, dependency maps, and entropy analysis. Includes a full CLI with persistent stats tracking and one-command setup. Single Rust binary, zero dependencies.

Better reasoning. Longer sessions. Lower costs.

89–99% tokens
removed
50+ CLI
patterns
8 MCP
tools
Get Started Star on GitHub
LeanCTX metrics

$ ctx_metrics

LeanCTX session metrics

══════════════════════════════════════════════════

Files: 12 tracked | Reads: 34 | Hits: 22 (65%)

Tokens: 28.5K → 4.1K sent | 24.3K saved (85%)

Cost: $0.427 → $0.062 | $0.365 saved

──────────────────────────────────────────────────

Tool Calls Saved Avg%

ctx_read 18 19.2K 91%

ctx_shell 8 3.1K 74%

ctx_tree 4 1.2K 40%

The problem

AI coding tools were not built to be token-efficient

Every file read, every CLI output, every project scan — sent in full, every time. The cost adds up fast.

~65%

Redundant context

of file reads in a coding session are re-reads. Your AI agent receives the same content over and over, wasting tokens on context it already has.

$20–200

Token budget burns fast

per month — and every AI tool has hard limits. 500 requests, 45 messages per 5 hours, limited credits. Once you hit the cap, you wait.

200K

Worse reasoning

token context windows fill up with noise. The more irrelevant tokens the model processes, the lower the quality of its reasoning and code output.

Live comparison

See the difference

Real outputs from real projects. Side-by-side, token-counted.

Without LeanCTX
~3,517 tokens 
$ Read auth.ts
import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
import { db } from './db.js';

const SESSION_EXPIRY = 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;

export function generateToken(): string {
  return createHash('sha256')
    .update(Math.random().toString())
    .digest('hex');
}

export async function createUser(
  email: string, password: string,
  name: string, locale: string
) {
  const hash = await hashPassword(password);
  const token = generateToken();
  // ... 123 lines total
With LeanCTX
~13 tokens -99% 
$ ctx_read auth.ts
F1 [cached 2t 123L ∅]
Without LeanCTX
~2,536 tokens 
$ Read db.ts
import { Pool } from 'pg';
import { drizzle } from 'drizzle-orm/node-postgres';
import * as schema from './schema.js';

const pool = new Pool({
  connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL,
  max: 20,
  idleTimeoutMillis: 30000,
});

export const db = drizzle(pool, { schema });

export async function query<T>(
  sql: string, params: unknown[]
): Promise<T[]> {
  const result = await pool.query(sql, params);
  return result.rows as T[];
}
// ... 89 lines of implementation
With LeanCTX
~252 tokens -90% 
$ ctx_read db.ts --mode signatures
F2 [89L +]
fn ⊛ query<T>(sql:s, params:unknown[]) → T[]
fn ⊛ migrate()
fn ⊛ transaction<T>(fn:(tx) → T) → T
fn ⊛ healthCheck() → boolean
cl Database
  ⊛ connect()
  ⊛ disconnect()
[2284 tok saved (90%)]
Without LeanCTX
~847 tokens 
$ npm install express

added 65 packages, and audited 66 packages in 2s

12 packages are looking for funding
  run `npm fund` for details

found 0 vulnerabilities

npm warn deprecated inflight@1.0.6: This module is
not supported, and leaks memory. Do not use it. Check
out lru-cache if you want a good and tested way to
coalesce async requests by a key value, which is much
more comprehensive and powerful.

npm warn deprecated glob@7.2.3: Glob versions prior
to v9 are no longer supported
With LeanCTX
~24 tokens -97% 
$ ctx_shell "npm install express"
+express@4.18.2 (65 deps, 2s)
[823 tok saved (97%)]
Without LeanCTX
~612 tokens 
$ git status
On branch feature/auth
Your branch is ahead of 'origin/feature/auth' by 3 commits.
  (use "git push" to publish your local commits)

Changes to be committed:
  (use "git restore --staged <file>..." to unstage)
	new file:   src/auth/session.ts
	modified:   src/auth/login.ts
	modified:   src/routes/api.ts

Changes not staged for commit:
  (use "git add <file>..." to update what will be committed)
  (use "git restore <file>..." to discard changes in working directory)
	modified:   src/auth/session.ts

Untracked files:
  (use "git add <file>..." to include in what will be committed)
	src/auth/session.test.ts
With LeanCTX
~78 tokens -87% 
$ ctx_shell "git status"
feature/auth ↑3
staged: +src/auth/session.ts ~src/auth/login.ts ~src/routes/api.ts
unstaged: ~src/auth/session.ts
untracked: src/auth/session.test.ts
[534 tok saved (87%)]
Without LeanCTX
~1,240 tokens 
$ tsc --noEmit
src/auth/session.ts(12,5): error TS2322: Type 'string | undefined'
is not assignable to type 'string'.
  Type 'undefined' is not assignable to type 'string'.

src/auth/session.ts(24,9): error TS2345: Argument of type 'number'
is not assignable to parameter of type 'string'.

src/routes/api.ts(8,3): error TS2739: Type '{ id: number; }'
is missing the following properties from type 'User':
name, email, createdAt

src/routes/api.ts(45,28): error TS2551: Property 'stauts' does
not exist on type 'Response'. Did you mean 'status'?

Found 4 errors in 2 files.

Errors  Files
     2  src/auth/session.ts:12
     2  src/routes/api.ts:8
With LeanCTX
~142 tokens -89% 
$ ctx_shell "tsc --noEmit"
4 errors in 2 files:
session.ts:12 TS2322 string|undefined ≠ string
session.ts:24 TS2345 number ≠ string
api.ts:8 TS2739 {id} missing name,email,createdAt
api.ts:45 TS2551 'stauts' → 'status'
[1098 tok saved (89%)]
Without LeanCTX
~980 tokens 
$ find src -type f | head -30
src/core/compressor.ts
src/core/config.ts
src/core/differ.ts
src/core/entropy-compressor.ts
src/core/protocol.ts
src/core/session-cache.ts
src/core/signature-extractor.ts
src/core/store.ts
src/core/token-counter.ts
src/dashboard/server.ts
src/dashboard/ui.ts
src/patterns/docker.ts
src/patterns/git.ts
src/patterns/index.ts
src/patterns/npm.ts
src/patterns/typescript.ts
src/tools/ctx-analyze.ts
src/tools/ctx-benchmark.ts
src/tools/ctx-compress.ts
...
With LeanCTX
~588 tokens -40% 
$ ctx_tree --depth 2
src/
  core/ (9)
  tools/ (7)
  dashboard/ (2)
  patterns/ (5)
  cli.ts
  index.ts
[392 tok saved (40%)]
Without LeanCTX
~28,450 tokens 
$ Read all 12 cached files again...

// auth.ts — 123 lines
import { createHash } from 'node:crypto';
...

// db.ts — 89 lines
import { Pool } from 'pg';
...

// routes.ts — 234 lines
import { Router } from 'express';
...

// ... 9 more files, full content
// Total: 28,450 tokens re-sent
With LeanCTX
~890 tokens -97% 
$ ctx_compress
CTX CHECKPOINT (12 files)

F1 auth.ts [123L]: fn generateToken,
  fn createUser, fn verifyEmail, ...
F2 db.ts [89L]: cl Database, fn query,
  fn migrate
F3 routes.ts [234L]: fn setupRoutes,
  fn authMiddleware

STATS: 34 reads, 22 hits (65%)
COMPRESSION: 28450 → 890 tok (97%)
Without LeanCTX
~1,856 tokens 
$ Read middleware.ts
import express from 'express';
import type { Request, Response, NextFunction } from 'express';

/**
 * @description Authentication middleware
 * @param {Request} req - Express request
 * @param {Response} res - Express response
 * @param {NextFunction} next - Next function
 * @returns {void}
 */
export function authMiddleware(
  req: Request,
  res: Response,
  next: NextFunction
): void {
  const token = req.headers.authorization;
  if (!token) {
    res.status(401).json({ error: 'Unauthorized' });
    return;
  }
  // ... 78 lines, mostly JSDoc + boilerplate
With LeanCTX
~312 tokens -83% 
$ ctx_read middleware.ts --mode entropy
F8 [78L +] (H̄=3.2 bits/char)
⊘ 14 low-entropy lines (H<2.0)
⊘ 3 duplicate patterns (J≥0.7)
fn authMiddleware(req,res,next)
  token = req.headers.authorization
  if !token → 401
  verify(token) → req.user
fn rateLimiter(max,window)
  ...
[1544 tok saved (83%)]

The toolkit

8 MCP Tools + CLI. One Goal.

Every tool minimizes tokens while maximizing context quality. Measured with tiktoken, benchmarked on real projects.

ctx_read

Smart File Read

6 modes: cached reads, dependency maps, signatures-only, diff-updates, entropy filtering, and aggressive compression. Re-reads cost 13 tokens instead of thousands.

89-99% savings

ctx_shell

CLI Compression

50+ compression patterns across 12 categories — git, npm, cargo, docker, kubectl, gh, pip, ruff, and more. Also available as transparent shell hook.

60-90% savings

ctx_tree

Project Map

Token-efficient directory listings using indentation and file counts. The LLM sees structure without reading every file path.

40% savings

ctx_compress

Context Checkpoint

Compresses all cached files into an ultra-compact signature map. Memory checkpoint for long conversations.

90-95% savings

ctx_analyze

Entropy Analysis

Shannon entropy distribution + Jaccard pattern analysis. Shows which lines carry real information and recommends the optimal mode.

Diagnostic savings

ctx_benchmark

Measure Savings

Benchmarks any file against every strategy with tiktoken. See exact token counts for data-driven mode selection.

Exact counts savings

ctx_metrics

Session Stats

tiktoken-measured savings per session. Track cache hit rates, compression ratios, and cumulative totals across tools.

Real-time savings

ctx_search

Code Search

Search for patterns across files and return only matching lines with compact context. No need to read entire files.

50-80% savings

lean-ctx init

One-Command Setup

Installs 23 shell aliases globally with --global. Adds LeanCTX compression to git, npm, cargo, docker, kubectl, gh, pip, ruff, and more.

Auto-setup savings

lean-ctx gain

Savings Dashboard

Shows cumulative token savings across all sessions. Persistent stats tracked in ~/.lean-ctx/stats.json. See your lifetime ROI at a glance.

Persistent savings
Explore all tools

Universal compatibility

No AI tool offers unlimited usage.
Every token counts.

Whether you pay per request, per credit, or per token — LeanCTX compresses context so your limits stretch further.

~40

file reads per session

~85K

tokens of context noise

~9K

with LeanCTX (89% less)

Price

$20 — $200/mo

Limits

500 fast, then on-demand

Context

Up to 200K tokens

Credits stretch ~3x further

Price

Limits

Context

Up to 200K tokens

$20 — $200/mo

~45 msgs/5h (Pro)

Context

200K tokens

Sessions ~3x longer

Limits

500 credits/mo (Pro)

Context

200K tokens

Credits last ~2x longer

Price

$10 — $50/mo

Limits

Credit-based

Context

Up to 128K tokens

More completions per credit

Cline

Price

Bring your API key

Limits

Pay per token (API)

Context

200K tokens

Direct API cost reduction

Continue

Price

Free + API costs

Limits

Pay per token (API)

Context

200K tokens

Fewer tokens per autocomplete

Aider

Price

Free + API costs

Limits

Pay per token (API)

Context

Lower per-session API bills

How we compare

Session caching, signature extraction, context compression, and diff-based updates deliver savings no other tool can match.

Feature LeanCTX RTK OrbitalMCP
Architecture Hybrid: Shell Hook + MCP Shell hook only Chat panel
Language Rust (single binary) Rust TypeScript
Token Savings 89-99% 60-90% CLI only 20-25%
CLI Patterns 50+ (12 categories) ~30 commands None
Scope Files + CLI + Search + Deps + grep/find/ls/curl Shell output Chat panel
Session Caching MD5-based None None
Read Modes 6 (full, map, signatures, diff, aggressive, entropy) None None
Signature Engine tree-sitter AST (10 languages) None None
Dependency Maps Import/export extraction (10 languages via tree-sitter) None None
Diff Mode Line-level diffs None None
Context Compress Checkpoint snapshots None None
Token Counting tiktoken-exact (o200k_base) Estimated None
Cost Tracking USD estimates per session + persistent lifetime stats None None
Token Dense Dialect TDD mode: symbol shorthand (8-25% extra) None None
Thinking Reduction CRP v2 (30-60% fewer thinking tokens) None None
Config File TOML (~/.lean-ctx/config.toml) TOML None
History Analysis lean-ctx discover None None
Auto-Setup lean-ctx init --global (23 aliases) Manual Manual
Homebrew brew tap yvgude/lean-ctx Yes None
CLI Commands gain, init, read, diff, grep, find, ls, deps, discover, session, config None None
Persistent Stats Lifetime savings + web dashboard None None
Multi-Editor Cursor, Copilot, Claude Code, Windsurf + Shell Claude Code Cursor only
Open Source Yes (MIT) Yes (MIT) No

Get started

Running in 60 seconds

No cloud account. No API keys. No subscription. Install, configure, done.

1

Install

Single Rust binary via Homebrew or crates.io

# Homebrew (macOS / Linux)
brew tap yvgude/lean-ctx && brew install lean-ctx

# Or via crates.io
cargo install lean-ctx
2

Set Up Shell Hook

Installs 23 aliases for git, npm, docker, kubectl, gh, and more

lean-ctx init --global
3

Configure Your Editor

Add LeanCTX to your MCP configuration

~/.cursor/mcp.json

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lean-ctx": {
      "command": "lean-ctx"
    }
  }
}

.github/copilot/mcp.json

{
  "servers": {
    "lean-ctx": {
      "command": "lean-ctx"
    }
  }
}

Terminal

claude mcp add lean-ctx lean-ctx

~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json

{
  "mcpServers": {
    "lean-ctx": {
      "command": "lean-ctx"
    }
  }
}
4

Watch Your Savings

Every read is now optimized

$ ctx_metrics

LeanCTX session metrics
Files tracked: 12 | Reads: 34 | Cache hits: 22 (65%)
Input tokens: 28.5K → 4.1K sent | 24.3K saved (85%)
Cost estimate: $0.427 → $0.062 | $0.365 saved

Your AI doesn't need to
read all that.

Get started with LeanCTX. Better reasoning, longer sessions, lower costs.
